studyDescription: Blind taste tests across 10 dairy categories with 2,183 omnivore and flexitarian consumers, conducted with Palate Insights.
headline: Taste parity is becoming a reality across multiple dairy categories — Califia Farms Oat Barista Blend reached parity with Horizon Whole Milk in hot latte.

categories.name: Barista milk
categories.blurb: The category that broke through. Oat baristas hold foam, sweeten lattes, and now match dairy in blind tests.

picks.note: Reached taste parity with Horizon Whole Milk in Nectar's blind hot-latte test.
Industry standard in third-wave cafés; steams to silk.
Higher protein for protein-forward menus.

categories.name: Milk
categories.blurb: The best-tasting category overall — and 15× more market share than cheese, the worst.
categories.leader: Top performers cluster within 0.4pts of dairy

picks.note: Closest macros to whole milk; 8g protein per cup.
Best body for cold cereal and baking.
Lowest cost per gallon at scale.

categories.name: Butter (salted)
categories.blurb: Cultured plant butters now hit dairy notes for the table — sauté and laminating still need a recipe-side calibration.

picks.note: Cultured cashew base; closest to high-end European.
Workhorse foodservice block; ~$3.50/lb wholesale.
Pastry-friendly water content for laminating.

categories.name: Cheddar
categories.blurb: Sliced cheddar has caught up enough that grilled-cheese melts and burger toppers work without apology.

picks.brand: Violife Just Like Mature Cheddar Block
picks.note: Best slice for melts; coconut oil base.
Foodservice mainstay; melts predictably on the line.

picks.brand: Follow Your Heart Sharp Cheddar Slices
picks.note: Sharper finish for cold sandwiches.

categories.name: Mozzarella
categories.blurb: The toughest category — no TASTY winners. Pizzas need a binder + char strategy to hide the gap.

picks.note: Ladle-and-bake; best stretch in the category to date.
Workhorse shred for back-of-house pizza programs.
Newer entrant focused on pizzeria performance.

categories.name: Cream cheese
categories.blurb: Bagel-shop ready. Cashew and almond bases hold their shape and tang within striking distance of dairy.

picks.note: Almond milk base; most natural tang.
Long-standing foodservice option; soy-based.
Cultured cashew base; spreads cold.

categories.name: Creamer
categories.blurb: One of the most competitive categories — leaders within 0.2pts of dairy.

picks.note: Half-and-half body; the category's headline.
Workhorse foodservice creamer.
Unsweetened option for chefs cutting added sugar.

categories.name: Sour cream
categories.blurb: Drop-in for taco bars and bake stations. Cashew bases give you the cleanest tang.

picks.note: Cleanest tang; line-ready out of the tub.
Classic foodservice option; wide distribution.
Almond base; thicker body for dolloping.

categories.name: Yogurt
categories.blurb: Plain yogurts have arrived. Greek-style cashew and coconut bases pull through tang and thickness for parfaits and sauces.

picks.note: Brand parity is the kicker — same name, same shelf, same expectations.
Plain unsweetened tang; sauces and dips.
Thickest body for parfaits and savory swaps.

categories.name: Ice cream
categories.blurb: Premium oat and cashew pints now scoop and finish like dairy — the dessert tier is no longer a compromise.

picks.note: Premium dessert tier; oat or cashew base depending on flavor.
Mid-priced; consistent across the menu.
Wide foodservice availability; cashewmilk gives the cleanest dairy mouthfeel.

headlineWinner.claim: Reached taste parity with Horizon Whole Milk in blind hot-latte testing.
